2605.06334.title: MANTRA: Synthesizing SMT-Validated Compliance Benchmarks for Tool-Using LLM Agents
2605.06334.paper_summary: Tool-using large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in settings where their reliable behavior is governed by strict procedural manuals. Ensuring that such agents comply with the rules from these manuals is challenging, as they are typically written for humans in natural language while agent behavior manifests as an execution trace of tool calls. Existing evaluations of LLM agents rely on manually constructed benchmarks or LLM-based judges, which either do not scale or lack reliability for complex, long-horizon manuals. To overcome these limitations, we present MANTRA, a framework for automatically synthesizing machine-checkable compliance benchmarks from natural-language manuals and tool schemas. MANTRA independently generates (i) a symbolic world model capturing procedural dependencies, and (ii) a set of trace-level compliance checks for a given task, and validates their consistency using SMT solving. A structured repair loop resolves inconsistencies, requiring human intervention only as a fallback. %This yields benchmarks that are formally validated. Importantly, MANTRA supports arbitrary domains and long procedural manuals, and provides a tunable notion of task complexity which is utilized to automatically derive challenging tasks accompanying compliance checks. Using MANTRA, we build a new benchmark suite with 285 tasks across 6 domains scaling to 50+ page manuals with minimal human effort. Empirically, we show that the compliance checks are richer with stronger constraint enforcement compared to existing benchmarks. Additionally, the granularity of the checks can be used for debugging the agents' failure modes. These results demonstrate that combining automated benchmark generation with formally grounded validation methods enables scalable and reliable benchmarking of tool-using agents.
2605.06334.paper_summary_zh: 工具使用的大型語言模型（LLM）代理在其可靠行為受到嚴格程序手冊約束的環境中越來越多地被部署。確保這些代理遵循手冊中的規則是具有挑戰性的，因為這些手冊通常是以自然語言為人類撰寫的，而代理的行為則表現為工具調用的執行痕跡。現有的LLM代理評估依賴於手動構建的基準或基於LLM的評判，這些基準要麼無法擴展，要麼對於複雜的長期手冊缺乏可靠性。為了克服這些限制，我們提出了MANTRA，一個從自然語言手冊和工具模式自動合成機器可檢查合規基準的框架。MANTRA獨立生成（i）捕捉程序依賴性的符號世界模型，以及（ii）針對給定任務的一組追蹤級合規檢查，並使用SMT求解驗證其一致性。一個結構化的修復循環解決不一致性，僅在必要時需要人類干預作為後備。%這產生了經過正式驗證的基準。重要的是，MANTRA支持任意領域和長程序手冊，並提供可調的任務複雜性概念，這可用於自動推導伴隨合規檢查的挑戰性任務。使用MANTRA，我們建立了一個新的基準套件，包含跨越6個領域的285個任務，擴展到50頁以上的手冊，並且人力投入最小。實證表明，與現有基準相比，合規檢查的豐富性更高，約束執行更強。此外，檢查的粒度可用於調試代理的失敗模式。這些結果表明，將自動基準生成與正式基礎的驗證方法結合起來，可以實現工具使用代理的可擴展和可靠的基準測試。
2605.06334.author: Ashwani Anand et.al.
2605.06334.authors: Ashwani Anand, Ivi Chatzi, Ritam Raha, Anne-Kathrin Schmuck
